jokes.text: Very proud... minus.
jokes.setup: George Michael shows an A-minus grade and asks if his father is proud
jokes.punchline: Michael responds with 'Very proud... minus' - applying the grade format to his emotion
jokes.explanation: Perfect wordplay that's both mathematically logical and emotionally cutting. Shows Michael's perfectionist nature while being genuinely clever.

jokes.text: C- minus. 'C' M-I-N-E-S.
jokes.setup: Continuing the grade conversation format established in previous joke
jokes.punchline: Michael treats 'C-minus' as a spelling word, spelling out 'C MINES' instead of 'MINUS'
jokes.explanation: Good follow-up that extends the grade wordplay concept, though less sharp than the first one.

jokes.text: Wow. How impressive is that?
jokes.setup: George Michael admits he didn't study for his poor grade
jokes.punchline: Michael sarcastically treats not studying as an achievement
jokes.explanation: Standard sarcastic parent response, gets a chuckle but nothing special.

jokes.text: The bar for the refrigerator has been lowered, huh?
jokes.setup: Lindsay defends Maeby's poor grades as meaningless
jokes.punchline: Michael uses 'bar' literally (for the fridge) to comment on lowered standards
jokes.explanation: Clever visual/verbal pun connecting lowered standards to actual lowered bar, but somewhat forced.

jokes.text: Minus. An 'A' gets him ice cream. He knows that.
jokes.setup: Lindsay says Michael gives George Michael stress even when he gets an A
jokes.punchline: Michael corrects 'A-minus' and explains his reward system
jokes.explanation: Continues the obsessive grade-correction pattern while revealing Michael's rigid reward system.

jokes.text: And that's why you always leave a note.
jokes.setup: Elaborate scenario with fake blood and prosthetic arm falling off
jokes.punchline: The 'lesson' is revealed to be about something as mundane as leaving notes
jokes.explanation: Peak Arrested Development absurdism - massively disproportionate response to minor infraction. Becomes iconic catchphrase and sets template for the episode.

jokes.text: I thought he was trying to get us off dairy.
jokes.setup: Michael explains the lessons worked and they still leave notes
jokes.punchline: Buster completely misunderstood the point of the milk-related lesson
jokes.explanation: Perfect Buster moment - shows his childlike confusion and establishes his character. The misdirection is earned and character-specific.

jokes.text: I might have been just a little too believable.
jokes.setup: Gob explains he owes Hot Cops money for a gig
jokes.punchline: Massive understatement - he was so convincing as a cop he presumably arrested real criminals
jokes.explanation: Great understatement humor typical of Gob's oblivious narcissism. The implication is funnier than the statement.

jokes.text: I keep forgetting how hilarious you are.
jokes.setup: Michael makes an ATM joke when Gob asks for money
jokes.punchline: Gob's deadpan sarcastic response to Michael's dad joke
jokes.explanation: Good sibling dynamic - Gob's sarcasm works but the setup ATM joke is weak.

jokes.text: My gut is telling me 'No,' but my gut is also very hungry.
jokes.setup: Michael offers money in exchange for a future favor
jokes.punchline: Gob plays on 'gut feeling' literally - his hungry stomach overrules his instincts
jokes.explanation: Solid wordplay that reveals Gob's shallow decision-making process. Well-constructed pun with character insight.

jokes.text: We ought to put that on our family crest.
jokes.setup: Lucille immediately asks for a favor upon seeing Michael
jokes.punchline: Michael notes the pattern of family members constantly asking for favors
jokes.explanation: Good observational humor about family dynamics. Sets up expectation that will pay off throughout episode.

jokes.text: Not after we doctor all these receipts.
jokes.setup: Lucille claims no new purchases were made with company money
jokes.punchline: Immediately admits to planning to falsify documents
jokes.explanation: Perfect Lucille moment - casual admission of fraud delivered as an afterthought. Great character work.

jokes.text: I did refer to it as our nausea.
jokes.setup: Michael questions Buster's commitment to his relationship with Lucille 2
jokes.punchline: Buster admits to inappropriately co-opting his girlfriend's medical condition
jokes.explanation: Wonderfully uncomfortable Buster moment that reveals his boundary issues and neediness.

jokes.text: Well, now it's my nausea.
jokes.setup: Buster explains he called it 'our nausea' when things were hot and heavy
jokes.punchline: Now that the relationship has cooled, he's claimed full ownership of her symptom
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation of the previous joke. Buster's possessiveness over someone else's illness is peak uncomfortable comedy.

jokes.text: Because I'm... Don't want to.
jokes.setup: Lucille asks why Lindsay can't help catalog valuables
jokes.punchline: Lindsay starts to make an excuse, gives up, and just states she doesn't want to
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Lindsay's laziness and inability to even construct a proper excuse.

jokes.text: Sometimes a diet is the best defense.
jokes.setup: Lindsay worries about being criticized around-the-clock
jokes.punchline: Lucille immediately criticizes her weight while claiming she doesn't criticize
jokes.explanation: Classic Lucille - immediately contradicts herself with a cutting remark. Perfect timing and character consistency.

jokes.text: This is a D-minus. Either way, it's above a 'D,' right?
jokes.setup: Maeby presents a D-plus grade, Lindsay corrects it to D-minus
jokes.punchline: Maeby's twisted logic that D-minus is still above D
jokes.explanation: Good character work showing Maeby's manipulative intelligence and mathematical creativity.

jokes.text: Is 'Lindsay' with an 'A' or an 'E'?
jokes.setup: Maeby deciding to forge Lindsay's signature on her report card
jokes.punchline: Maeby doesn't know how to spell her own mother's name
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly absurd character moment - shows the family dysfunction in one line

jokes.text: I know you got a 'crocodile' in spelling, but this has gone too far.
jokes.setup: Lindsay tries to be stern about Maeby forging her signature
jokes.punchline: Lindsay uses 'crocodile' instead of 'alligator' (meaning a low grade), showing her own academic struggles
jokes.explanation: Nice malapropism that shows Lindsay's own educational deficits while she tries to parent Maeby.

jokes.text: Hmm. Nope, that doesn't feel right.
jokes.setup: Lindsay attempts to discipline Maeby with authority
jokes.punchline: Immediately second-guesses herself, abandoning the parental stance
jokes.explanation: Perfect Lindsay character moment - she can't sustain any authoritative parenting for more than seconds.

jokes.text: I punish thee.
jokes.setup: Lindsay tries again to discipline Maeby after changing her mind
jokes.punchline: Uses formal, archaic language like pronouncing a royal decree
jokes.explanation: Funny incongruity between Lindsay's modern suburban parenting and medieval pronouncement style.

jokes.text: Oh, I have to come up with another thing?
jokes.setup: Maeby asks what the punishment actually is
jokes.punchline: Lindsay realizes she hasn't thought beyond the declaration of punishment
jokes.explanation: Great character comedy showing Lindsay's complete inability to follow through on parenting decisions.

jokes.text: Sleeves.
jokes.setup: Lucille says she has one word for Lindsay
jokes.punchline: The devastating criticism is simply 'Sleeves' - commenting on Lindsay's sleeveless outfit
jokes.explanation: Perfect Lucille moment - maximum passive-aggressive impact with minimum words. The single-word criticism is devastatingly effective.

jokes.text: Dumb, dumb. Dumb, George Michael.
jokes.setup: George Michael is frustrated with a math problem
jokes.punchline: He calls himself dumb repeatedly in a self-flagellating manner
jokes.explanation: Shows George Michael's perfectionist anxiety and self-punishment, setting up Michael's concern about pressure.

jokes.text: Your finger is still in the book.
jokes.setup: Michael tells George Michael to close the book and relax
jokes.punchline: George Michael can't even fully commit to closing the book - keeps his finger as a bookmark
jokes.explanation: Great visual metaphor for George Michael's inability to truly relax or let go of academic pressure.

jokes.text: Our. I mean, our... our nausea.
jokes.setup: Buster catches himself saying 'your nausea' when asking about Lucille 2
jokes.punchline: Corrects himself to maintain the shared ownership of her medical condition
jokes.explanation: Nice callback that shows Buster still clinging to the possessive relationship with Lucille 2's illness.

jokes.text: Buster was found two days later trying to eat his way through this enormous plaster donut.
jokes.setup: Narrator explains Buster participated in marijuana study involving carnival
jokes.punchline: Visual of Buster literally trying to eat through a giant decorative donut while high
jokes.explanation: Brilliant visual gag that perfectly captures Buster's childlike nature and the absurd lengths the show goes to for backstory.

jokes.text: Dad told me to take the day off, so I decided to come into work.
jokes.setup: Buster finds George Michael at the banana stand
jokes.punchline: George Michael's idea of taking a day off is to go to his job instead of school
jokes.explanation: Perfect George Michael character work - his workaholic nature means even rebellion involves more work.

jokes.text: She's your girlfriend? Dad said you were her nurse.
jokes.setup: Buster claiming Lucille 2 as his girlfriend
jokes.punchline: George Michael's innocent observation that reveals how others see the relationship
jokes.explanation: Good character moment highlighting the inappropriate nature of Buster's relationship

jokes.text: I could put it on a tea biscuit, tell her it's a marshmallow.
jokes.setup: Buster explains he wants marijuana pills for his girlfriend's nausea
jokes.punchline: His plan to deceive her about medication by disguising it as candy
jokes.explanation: Darkly funny plan that shows Buster's misguided attempts to help while revealing his deceptive tendencies.

jokes.text: This place is pot central, right?
jokes.setup: Buster assumes George Michael can get drugs because he works at the banana stand
jokes.punchline: The innocent frozen banana stand is characterized as a drug den
jokes.explanation: Good irony between the wholesome banana stand and Buster's assumptions about boardwalk drug culture.

jokes.text: Big yellow joint. The big yellow joint. I'll meet you down at the big yellow joint.
jokes.setup: Narrator explains the banana's resemblance to a marijuana cigarette inspired a song
jokes.punchline: Song lyrics about the 'big yellow joint' (banana) sound like drug references
jokes.explanation: Clever double entendre that works both as innocent banana reference and drug slang. Becomes a running musical gag.

jokes.text: Why are you hitting yourself? Why are you hitting yourself?
jokes.setup: Scene transition to George Michael and Maeby
jokes.punchline: They're doing the classic schoolyard 'hitting yourself' game, mimicking the J. Walter Weatherman scenario
jokes.explanation: Nice callback showing how the family's bizarre teaching methods have influenced the kids' play.

jokes.text: Here. Take 225 from me.
jokes.setup: Michael gives George Michael $20 to go make mistakes and be young
jokes.punchline: Buster dramatically escalates with $225, completely missing the point of the lesson
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Buster's inability to understand appropriate scale and his desperate need for George Michael's help.

jokes.text: Go make mistakes, get in trouble... be young.
jokes.setup: Michael telling George Michael to take the day off
jokes.punchline: Encouraging his son to be irresponsible, which is the opposite of Michael's usual approach
jokes.explanation: Good ironic moment showing Michael's attempt to be less controlling

jokes.text: And this is from when your Pop-Pop yelled out, 'Oh, Melanie...' when he was making love to Gangy.
jokes.setup: Lucille is showing Maeby family photos
jokes.punchline: Casually reveals grandfather's affair through inappropriate oversharing with grandchild
jokes.explanation: Perfectly inappropriate Lucille moment - treating family scandals as casual conversation with a teenager.

jokes.text: This is so much fun. I can't believe my mom thought being here would be a punishment.
jokes.setup: Maeby is hearing inappropriate family secrets
jokes.punchline: She genuinely enjoys the dysfunction that was meant to discipline her
jokes.explanation: Great irony showing how Lindsay's punishment backfired because Maeby thrives on family drama and gossip.

jokes.text: Oh. Pretty. It'll distract from the freckles.
jokes.setup: Maeby tries on a brooch that Lucille offers her
jokes.punchline: Lucille gives a backhanded compliment, simultaneously offering jewelry and criticizing Maeby's appearance
jokes.explanation: Classic Lucille passive-aggression - manages to insult her granddaughter while appearing generous.

jokes.text: 100. I accidentally said 200. I don't know...
jokes.setup: Gob claims George Michael left him $200 in the note, Michael corrects it to $100
jokes.punchline: Gob awkwardly admits he misspoke about the amount, implying he kept extra money
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Gob's petty theft and inability to cover it up smoothly.

jokes.text: Really, a hundred dollars. You can't get good weed for a hundred bucks.
jokes.setup: Gob is trying to defend the drug purchase
jokes.punchline: Casually reveals his expertise in drug pricing while being oblivious to the family crisis
jokes.explanation: Classic Gob missing the point while accidentally revealing his own drug knowledge.

jokes.text: Maybe it was the other George Michael... the singer-songwriter.
jokes.setup: Michael asks if George Michael was at the docks
jokes.punchline: George Michael deflects by referencing the famous musician who shares his name
jokes.explanation: Clever deflection that shows George Michael's quick thinking while maintaining his innocent character.

jokes.text: Tell me before you hit the gas. I can't hear you! The kids are yelling. Tell me before you hit the gas.
jokes.setup: George Sr. demonstrates why you don't yell using J. Walter Weatherman
jokes.punchline: The escalating confusion leads to the car accident that 'causes' Weatherman to lose his arm
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation showing how the elaborate lesson scenarios were staged, with perfect absurd logic.

jokes.text: And that's why you don't yell.
jokes.setup: Elaborate car accident scenario with fake blood and prosthetics
jokes.punchline: The lesson reveal follows the exact same format as the note lesson
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback that establishes the formula while showing the scope of George Sr.'s elaborate teaching methods.

jokes.text: You killed him when you left the door open with the air conditioner running.
jokes.setup: Michael asks how to contact J. Walter Weatherman, George Sr. says he's dead
jokes.punchline: Blames Michael for Weatherman's death through another mundane household mistake
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark humor that suggests George Sr. continued the lessons even into adulthood with deadly consequences.

jokes.text: Dad, that's just... That's one night, and it's back in September.
jokes.setup: George Sr. claims it's Yom Kippur
jokes.punchline: Michael corrects his father's obviously wrong Jewish holiday timing
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing George Sr.'s fake religious conversion and Michael's exasperation.

jokes.text: You've only been a Jew about two days.
jokes.setup: George Sr. tries to use religious obligations to avoid helping
jokes.punchline: Michael points out the recent and superficial nature of the conversion
jokes.explanation: Sharp observation about George Sr.'s convenient religious timing with good deadpan delivery.

jokes.text: It's Tuesday. Shh, shh, shh.
jokes.setup: George Sr. claims he needs to prepare for Sabbath
jokes.punchline: Michael points out it's Tuesday; George Sr. shushes him to maintain the religious facade
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of George Sr.'s fake religious observance with the shushing being a nice character touch.

jokes.text: That was supposed to be for me. She was my au pair.
jokes.setup: Lindsay sees Maeby wearing the brooch Lucille gave her
jokes.punchline: Lindsay reveals she had an affair with the au pair and expected inheritance
jokes.explanation: Dark family secret revealed through inheritance dispute, typical of the show's casual approach to dysfunction.

jokes.text: I know, but it's an elephant... and I didn't want to invite the comparison.
jokes.setup: Lindsay complains she wanted the elephant brooch
jokes.punchline: Lucille explains she didn't give it to Lindsay because of the elephant/weight comparison
jokes.explanation: Devastatingly cruel Lucille moment that's both visually and verbally cutting. Perfect character consistency.

jokes.text: That chubby little wrist of yours... is testing the tensile strength of this bracelet as it is.
jokes.setup: Maeby suggests they could go get ice cream together
jokes.punchline: Lucille cruelly rejects the idea by commenting on Maeby's weight in technical terms
jokes.explanation: Brutally cutting Lucille line that uses technical language to make the insult even more clinical and devastating.

jokes.text: I am like a caged animal. My old lady's on the floor, man.
jokes.setup: Buster finds George Michael and explains his desperate situation
jokes.punchline: Uses street slang inappropriately while describing his elderly girlfriend's medical condition
jokes.explanation: Great incongruity between Buster's sheltered nature and his attempt at tough-guy talk about his relationship.

jokes.text: Oh. Oh, I was talking about me. But, yeah, she's a mess.
jokes.setup: George Michael says Buster is making a miserable person happy
jokes.punchline: Buster clarifies he meant himself, then adds his girlfriend is also miserable
jokes.explanation: Perfect Buster self-centeredness disguised as altruism, with good misdirection structure.

jokes.text: Let's deal some drugs.
jokes.setup: Gob meets George Michael at the yacht for the fake drug deal
jokes.punchline: Gob delivers the line with dramatic flair, treating it like a real criminal enterprise
jokes.explanation: Classic Gob overplaying his role with theatrical criminal bravado inappropriate for the situation.

jokes.text: Drug delivery!
jokes.setup: They're waiting for the drug dealer to arrive by boat
jokes.punchline: The 'dealer' arrives immediately and announces himself like a pizza delivery guy
jokes.explanation: Good absurd humor treating drug dealing like legitimate business with customer service announcements.

jokes.text: Okay, you can put your shirt down, Derek. Just give him the stuff.
jokes.setup: The Hot Cop is showing off his physique during the drug deal
jokes.punchline: Gob has to remind the stripper to stay focused and not strip during the fake crime
jokes.explanation: Perfect visual comedy as the strippers can't help but do their job even during the fake drug bust.

jokes.text: Let's get this party started.
jokes.setup: The fake police officers arrive for the 'bust'
jokes.punchline: Instead of police commands, they use party/strip club language
jokes.explanation: Good comedy from the strippers being unable to maintain their police character and reverting to entertainer mode.

jokes.text: I was gonna smoke the marijuana like a cigarette.
jokes.setup: Michael pressures George Michael to admit the drugs were for him
jokes.punchline: George Michael's innocent description of drug use shows his complete inexperience
jokes.explanation: Perfect George Michael innocence - his clinical description of smoking shows he has no real knowledge of drugs.

jokes.text: Why don't you just wait it out? She's gonna be gone soon.
jokes.setup: Buster explains the marijuana was for his sick girlfriend
jokes.punchline: Michael casually suggests waiting for the elderly woman to die
jokes.explanation: Darkly funny practical suggestion that reveals Michael's pragmatic but insensitive approach to family problems.

jokes.text: Why are you hitting yourself? Why are you hitting yourself?
jokes.setup: Michael makes the insensitive comment about waiting out the girlfriend
jokes.punchline: Buster physically attacks Michael using the classic schoolyard move
jokes.explanation: Good physical comedy callback that shows how the family's bizarre teaching methods created this weird form of conflict resolution.

jokes.text: No, no! These are just strippers! Look how hot they are!
jokes.setup: Real police arrive and think the Hot Cops are drug dealers
jokes.punchline: Gob's defense is to point out how attractive the strippers are
jokes.explanation: Perfect Gob logic under pressure - his evidence that they're innocent is their physical attractiveness.

jokes.text: Oh, my God! This guy's arm just came off and landed on the dock.
jokes.setup: Chaos of the drug bust with multiple groups of people
jokes.punchline: J. Walter Weatherman loses his prosthetic arm again during the mayhem
jokes.explanation: Perfect culmination bringing back the signature visual gag in the chaos, with an outside observer's shocked reaction.

jokes.text: And that's why you don't teach lessons to your son.
jokes.setup: The elaborate fake lesson has completely backfired into real danger
jokes.punchline: George Sr. delivers a lesson about not teaching lessons, using the same format
jokes.explanation: Masterpiece of meta-comedy - uses the lesson format to teach against using the lesson format. Perfect recursive humor that comments on the entire episode structure.

jokes.text: So you taught me a lesson not to teach lessons? It was my last lesson.
jokes.setup: Michael realizes his father orchestrated another elaborate lesson
jokes.punchline: George Sr. admits it's a lesson about lessons, claiming it's his final one
jokes.explanation: Excellent meta-commentary on the recursive nature of the teaching and the episode's theme structure.

jokes.text: One of the Hot Cops is my choir teacher.
jokes.setup: George Michael reveals he knew about the fake bust the whole time
jokes.punchline: His innocent extracurricular activity connected him to the strip club
jokes.explanation: Perfect reveal that recontextualizes George Michael's behavior while maintaining his innocent character through the choir connection.

jokes.text: Like, say, I had a crush on my own cousin.
jokes.setup: Michael says George Michael can tell him anything
jokes.punchline: George Michael immediately confesses to incestuous feelings as a hypothetical
jokes.explanation: Perfectly uncomfortable moment that advances the ongoing George Michael/Maeby subplot while testing the limits of open communication.

jokes.text: You just taught me a lesson.
jokes.setup: George Michael's uncomfortable confession after Michael said he could say anything
jokes.punchline: Michael realizes he accidentally taught a lesson about not saying you can say 'anything'
jokes.explanation: Nice meta-moment where the lesson format continues even when Michael doesn't intend it, showing the cyclical nature of family patterns.

jokes.text: Big yellow joint. The big yellow joint. I'll meet you down at the big yellow joint.
jokes.setup: Episode ending with the family reconciled
jokes.punchline: The banana stand song plays again as they walk away
jokes.explanation: Nice bookend callback that ties the episode together thematically while providing musical closure.
